## Econ Release Check (Fri, Feb 27, 2026 — 10:00 AM ET)

At least one listed release **was published today**.

### 1) Producer Price Index (PPI) — **Released today**
- **Series:** BLS Producer Price Indexes (January 2026)
- **Official source:** https://www.bls.gov/news.release/ppi.htm  
- **Release timestamp (official):** **8:30 a.m. ET, Friday, February 27, 2026** (BLS PPI release header)
- **Checked:** Feb 27, 2026, ~10:01 a.m. ET

**Headline print**
- Final demand PPI: **+0.5% m/m** (BLS; ppi.htm)
- Prior: **+0.4% m/m (Dec 2025)** and **+0.2% m/m (Nov 2025)** (BLS; ppi.htm)
- Y/Y final demand: **+2.9%** (BLS; ppi.htm)
- Core proxy (final demand less foods, energy, and trade services): **+0.3% m/m**, **+3.4% y/y** (BLS; ppi.htm)

**Notable sub-components**
- Final demand **services +0.8% m/m** (largest since Jul 2025) (BLS; ppi.htm)
- Final demand **goods -0.3% m/m** (BLS; ppi.htm)
- Final demand **trade services +2.5% m/m** (BLS; ppi.htm)

**Direct quote**
- “**The Producer Price Index for final demand increased 0.5 percent in January…**” (BLS; ppi.htm)

**Confounding/context note**
- Nearby releases have been affected by compressed schedules tied to prior federal funding lapses; sequencing effects may add short-term noise to monthly comparisons (BLS release context across recent pages).

**Quick market interpretation**
- A firmer-than-recent PPI headline with sticky core/services pressure is a mild hawkish inflation signal for rates expectations; watch pass-through into upcoming CPI/PCE and margin sensitivity across rate-duration sectors.

---

**Economic Releases Today (Mar 11, 2026 ET): CPI**

### 1) Metadata
- **Series:** Consumer Price Index (CPI), February 2026
- **Official source:** https://www.bls.gov/news.release/cpi.htm
- **Release time (official):** **8:30 a.m. ET, Wednesday, March 11, 2026** (BLS CPI release page)
- **Accessed:** Mar 11, 2026, ~10:00 a.m. ET

### 2) Headline Print
- **CPI MoM (SA):** **+0.3%** (BLS)
- **CPI YoY (NSA):** **+2.4%** (BLS)
- **Core CPI MoM (SA):** **+0.2%** (BLS)
- **Core CPI YoY (NSA):** **+2.5%** (BLS)
- **Prior month (Jan 2026):** CPI MoM **+0.2%**, Core CPI MoM **+0.3%** (BLS table in same release)
- **Consensus:** Not published in the official BLS release itself; no official consensus figure available from the listed CPI source URL.

### 3) Notable Sub-Components
- **Shelter:** +0.2% MoM (largest contributor to monthly all-items increase)
- **Energy:** +0.6% MoM; gasoline +0.8% MoM
- **Food:** +0.4% MoM (food at home +0.4%; food away from home +0.3%)
- **Medical care services:** +0.6% MoM
- **Used cars & trucks:** -0.4% MoM
(All from BLS CPI release text/table)

### 4) Confounding / Contextual Factors
- BLS notes **October and November 2025 values are unavailable due to the 2025 lapse in appropriations**, which affects continuity in some historical monthly sequences.

### 5) Direct Quote(s)
- “The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased **0.3 percent** on a seasonally adjusted basis in February…”
- “Over the last 12 months, the all items index increased **2.4 percent** before seasonal adjustment.”
(BLS CPI release)

### 6) Market Interpretation (concise)
- This print suggests inflation is **still moderating in YoY terms** but with **sticky monthly pressure** from shelter and services.
- Core at +0.2% MoM is not an upside shock, but combined with firm shelter/services, it likely supports a **cautious Fed easing path** rather than aggressive near-term cuts.

### 7) Positioning Takeaway
- Net: inflation trend is improved vs prior peaks, but …

*Economic Releases Today (Fri, Mar 13, 2026 — ET)*

Three releases in the econ-release directory were published today and verified from official listed sources:

1) *BEA Personal Income & Outlays (Jan 2026)*
Source: https://www.bea.gov/news/2026/personal-income-and-outlays-january-2026
- Released: Mar 13, 2026 (BEA listing); accessed ~10:01 AM ET
- *Headline:* Personal income +0.4% m/m; DPI +0.9% m/m; PCE +0.4% m/m
- *Inflation details:* PCE price index +0.3% m/m, +2.8% y/y; Core PCE +0.4% m/m, +3.1% y/y
- *Notable internals:* Services spending +$105.7B, goods spending -$24.6B; saving rate 4.5%
- *Confounder noted by agency:* Release was rescheduled due to Oct–Nov 2025 federal shutdown

2) *BEA GDP (Second Estimate), Q4 2025*
Source: https://www.bea.gov/news/2026/gdp-second-estimate-4th-quarter-and-year-2025
- Released: Mar 13, 2026 (BEA listing); accessed ~10:01 AM ET
- *Headline:* Real GDP +0.7% SAAR (revised down from +1.4% advance)
- *Price details:* PCE prices +2.9% SAAR; Core PCE +2.7% SAAR
- *Notable internals:* Growth led by consumer spending and investment; partly offset by lower gov’t spending and exports
- *Confounders noted by agency:* Missing Oct CPI during shutdown was imputed; BEA estimates shutdown reduced Q4 real GDP growth by ~1.0 pp

3) *University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment (Prelim Mar 2026)*
Source: https://www.sca.isr.umich.edu/
- Posted as “Preliminary Results for March 2026”; accessed ~10:01 AM ET
- *Headline:* Sentiment 55.5 (Feb 56.6; Mar 2025 57.0)
- *Notable internals:* Current conditions 57.8; expectations 54.1; year-ahead inflation expectations 3.4%, long-run 3.2%
- *Direct quote/theme:* Joanne Hsu says sentiment dipped ~2% and cites gasoline prices + post–military-action-in-Iran deterioration in interviews

*Quick market take (concise):*

### Econ Release Check (Mon, Mar 16, 2026 | 10:00 AM ET)

At least one listed release **did occur today**:

## Federal Reserve — Industrial Production & Capacity Utilization (G.17)
- **Source URL:** https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/g17/current/
- **Official release date/time:** **March 16, 2026** (Federal Reserve page; standard release time listed in directory: 9:15 AM ET)
- **Accessed:** March 16, 2026 (America/New_York)

### Headline print
- **Industrial Production (Feb): +0.2% m/m** (Federal Reserve G.17; current release page)
- **Prior (Jan): +0.7% m/m** (Federal Reserve G.17; current release page)
- **Capacity utilization (Feb): 76.3%** (Federal Reserve G.17; current release page)
- **Prior (Jan): 76.3%** (Federal Reserve G.17; current release page)
- **Consensus:** Not published on the official Fed release page; no official consensus figure available from listed source URLs.

### Notable sub-components
- Manufacturing output: **+0.2% m/m**
- Mining output: **+0.8% m/m**
- Utilities output: **-0.6% m/m**
- Total IP level: **1.4% y/y** (vs Feb 2025)
- Capacity utilization remains **3.1pp below** long-run average (1972–2025)

### Direct quote
> “Industrial production (IP) increased 0.2 percent in February after moving up 0.7 percent in January.” (Federal Reserve G.17)

### Market interpretation (concise)
- Net read is **modestly growth-positive**: headline production rose, but the pace cooled from January.
- Flat utilization at **76.3%** suggests **limited near-term capacity pressure**, consistent with a non-overheating industrial backdrop.
- For asset allocation, this points to a **steady-but-not-accelerating** real-economy signal from U.S. industry.
